claims.claim: We investigate whether compiler and test failure evidence, organized as a curriculum of reusable failure-signature-to-repair-operator mappings, improves automated repair-loop recovery at a realistic multi-file workflow scale compared to a generic no-curriculum repair order.

claims.notes: Historical backfill linked the claim to already published artifacts; this is not independent source revalidation.

claims.claim: On 96 held-out evaluation cases across all 12 families, the baseline (fixed generic repair order) recovers 8/96 cases (0.083), while the error-curriculum strategy recovers 96/96 (1.000), yielding an absolute recovery lift of 0.917 and a relative lift of 11.0×.
